terms.definition: A GUI widget (e.g., button, text field) that the user manipulates to control the program.
terms.example: JButton go = new JButton("Go"); add(go, SOUTH);

terms.definition: ACM base class that provides a canvas for graphics and supports adding interactors around it.
terms.example: public class MyApp extends GraphicsProgram { public void run() { /* draw */ } }

terms.definition: Called before run(); used to build the UI (create components, add to window, register listeners).
terms.example: public void init() { add(new JButton("Start"), SOUTH); addActionListeners(); }

terms.definition: Main program logic that executes after the window is displayed and init() completes.
terms.example: public void run() { /* animation or core logic */ }

terms.definition: Adds an interactor to a layout region (NORTH, SOUTH, EAST, WEST) of the program window.
Layout model that positions interactors around the central canvas. NORTH/SOUTH create horizontal strips.
terms.example: add(new JLabel("Controls"), NORTH); add(new JButton("Go"), SOUTH);

terms.definition: Interface for receiving action events (e.g., button clicks, Enter in text fields).
terms.example: addActionListeners(); // Program listens for button actions

terms.definition: Callback invoked when an action occurs; write your response logic here.
Event object describing an action (source component, command string, etc.).
Clickable button interactor that fires an ActionEvent on click.
terms.example: JButton clear = new JButton("Clear"); add(clear, SOUTH);

terms.definition: Single-line text input. Pressing Enter can fire an ActionEvent if a listener is registered.
terms.example: JTextField tf = new JTextField(10); add(tf, SOUTH); tf.addActionListener(this);

terms.definition: Toggle interactor with checked/unchecked state; can use ItemListener or ActionListener.
terms.example: JCheckBox grid = new JCheckBox("Grid"); add(grid, SOUTH);

terms.definition: Mutually exclusive option when grouped via ButtonGroup; fires actions on selection.
terms.example: JRadioButton red = new JRadioButton("Red"); add(red, SOUTH);

terms.definition: Groups radio buttons so only one is selected at a time.
terms.example: ButtonGroup g = new ButtonGroup(); g.add(red); g.add(blue);

terms.definition: Drop-down list allowing one selection; can fire action events on selection change.
terms.example: JComboBox<String> shapes = new JComboBox<>(); shapes.addItem("Circle"); add(shapes, SOUTH);
